title: ext-session-lock-v1 protocol
notes: Implemented using smithay-client-toolkit SessionLockState/SessionLockHandler. LockApp struct with CompositorState, OutputState, SeatState, SessionLockState. Pending surfaces collected in locked(), wgpu surfaces created in configure(). Logs LOCKED on success, exits on LOCK DENIED. cargo check passes.
prompt: In crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs, implement the ext-session-lock-v1 Wayland protocol using smithay-client-toolkit and wayland-client. Steps: (1) connect to the Wayland display, get EventQueue and display proxy, (2) enumerate globals to find wl_compositor, wl_output, and ext_session_lock_manager_v1 (protocol name: 'ext_session_lock_manager_v1', version 1), (3) call ext_session_lock_manager_v1.lock() to get an ext_session_lock_v1 object, (4) for each wl_output, call ext_session_lock_v1.get_lock_surface(wl_surface, wl_output) to get an ext_session_lock_surface_v1, (5) handle the configure event on each lock surface: ack with ack_configure(serial) and commit the wl_surface, (6) in the event loop, handle the ext_session_lock_v1.locked event (log 'LOCKED') and finished event (log 'LOCK DENIED — exiting' and exit(1)). Define a LockState struct holding lock: ext_session_lock_v1, surfaces: Vec<LockSurface>. Use tracing for all logging. Import thermal_core::ThermalPalette. Dependencies: smithay-client-toolkit = '0.19', wayland-client = '0.31', thermal-core (local), tracing = '0.1', tracing-subscriber = '0.3'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.

title: wgpu surface setup
notes: WgpuSurface struct with wgpu::Surface<'static>, device, queue, config created in configure() using raw-window-handle Wayland pointers. Adapter requested with HighPerformance + compatible_surface. Surface configured with Bgra8UnormSrgb (or best available). render_frame() clears to ThermalPalette::BG. cargo check passes.
prompt: In crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs, add wgpu rendering to each LockSurface established in task 1. Steps: (1) after receiving the configure event with width/height, create a wgpu::Instance (backends: Vulkan | GL), (2) create a wgpu surface from the wl_surface using raw-window-handle Wayland display+surface pointers, (3) request adapter (power preference: HighPerformance), request device and queue, (4) configure the wgpu surface with format Bgra8UnormSrgb at the configure width/height, present_mode Fifo, (5) add a render_frame(&mut self) to LockSurface that gets a SurfaceTexture, creates a CommandEncoder, runs a RenderPass clearing to ThermalPalette::BG ([0.039, 0.0, 0.063, 1.0]), and submits+presents, (6) call render_frame() in the event loop on each surface each iteration. Store wgpu state (device, queue, surface, config) in LockSurface. Dependencies: wgpu = '24', thermal-core. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.

title: Heat-map shader
notes: WGSL fullscreen-triangle vertex shader + fragment shader with Voronoi-like sin/cos noise mapped through 6-stop thermal gradient (COOL→COLD→MILD→WARM→HOT→WHITE_HOT). TimeUniform (f32 + 12 bytes padding) updated each frame via write_buffer. HeatmapPipeline struct with bind group. Alpha 0.85. cargo check passes.
prompt: In crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs, add a custom wgpu render pipeline with a WGSL heat-map fragment shader. (1) Define a fullscreen-triangle vertex shader in WGSL (no vertex buffer — use vertex_index to emit clip-space positions covering the screen). (2) Write a fragment shader that: takes uv coordinates, computes a Voronoi-like noise pattern using sin/cos of position scaled by a time uniform, maps the noise value through the thermal gradient stops (COOL #1e3a8a → COLD #2d1b69 → MILD #0d9488 → WARM #22c55e → HOT #eab308 → WHITE_HOT #fef3c7) using mix(), clamps alpha to 0.85 so the bg color shows through slightly. (3) Create a wgpu::ShaderModule from the WGSL source string embedded via include_str! or a raw string literal. (4) Build a RenderPipeline with this shader, no vertex buffers, one color target matching the surface format. (5) Add a TimeUniform (f32 seconds since start) pushed via a uniform buffer updated each frame. (6) Replace the plain clear in render_frame() with this pipeline's draw call (3 vertices, 1 instance). Dependencies: wgpu = '24'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.

title: Auth input UI
notes: AuthState struct with password/failed/shake_timer. ThermalTextRenderer (from thermal-core) used for all text. 6 glyphon::Buffer objects: time, date, label, prompt, masked, denied. Masked input with blinking cursor (1Hz via elapsed%1.0). Buffers centered by estimated text width. AUTHENTICATE prompt in TEXT_MUTED, ACCESS DENIED in CRITICAL shown when failed=true. cargo check passes.
prompt: In crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs, add a password input field rendered with glyphon on top of the heat-map. (1) Add AuthState struct: password: String, masked: String (same length, all '●' chars), failed: bool, shake_timer: f32. (2) Initialize glyphon::FontSystem and glyphon::TextRenderer with the wgpu device. (3) In render_frame(), render the masked password string centered horizontally at 60% screen height using ThermalPalette::WARM (#22c55e) color, 24pt monospace. Render a prompt label 'AUTHENTICATE' above it in ThermalPalette::TEXT_MUTED (#7c6faa), 12pt. Render a blinking cursor ('|') appended to masked text — blink at 1Hz using time uniform mod 1.0. (4) Handle wl_keyboard key events: printable chars append to password, Backspace pops, Return triggers auth attempt (stub: call authenticate() returning bool). (5) If failed=true, render 'ACCESS DENIED' in ThermalPalette::CRITICAL (#dc2626) below the input. Dependencies: glyphon = '0.7', cosmic-text = '0.12'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.

title: PAM authentication
notes: auth.rs with direct FFI to libpam (no bindgen needed — avoids clang dependency). pam_start/pam_authenticate/pam_end called via unsafe extern C block. Conversation callback allocates response via malloc for PAM_PROMPT_ECHO_OFF messages. current_username() uses nix::unistd::User::from_uid(getuid()). build.rs emits cargo:rustc-link-lib=pam. workspace nix dep updated with 'user' feature. cargo check passes.
prompt: Create crates/thermal-lock/src/auth.rs. Implement PAM-based password authentication using the pam crate (add pam = '1' to crates/thermal-lock/Cargo.toml — note: this is not a workspace dep, add it directly). Implement: pub fn authenticate(username: &str, password: &str) -> bool. Steps: (1) create a pam::Authenticator with service name 'login', (2) set credentials with username and password, (3) call authenticator.authenticate() — return true on Ok, false on Err, (4) if Ok, call authenticator.open_session() for completeness then close it. Wrap errors with tracing::warn!. Also implement pub fn current_username() -> String using nix::unistd::getpwuid(nix::unistd::getuid()) to get the username from the passwd entry, falling back to env var USER or 'user'. Add pub mod auth; to main.rs and call auth::current_username() at startup. Dependencies: pam = '1' (add to Cargo.toml directly), nix = '0.29' (workspace), tracing = '0.1'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.

title: Clock display
notes: update_clock() method on WgpuSurface reads SystemTime::now(), computes HH:MM:SS and YYYY-MM-DD using pure std (custom days_to_date/is_leap). Updates buf_time and buf_date only when second changes (last_second tracking). Rendered at 35%/42%/28% screen height. THERMAL-LOCK label in ACCENT_COLD at 28%. cargo check passes.
prompt: In crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs, add a clock display rendered with glyphon. (1) In render_frame(), get current local time using std::time::SystemTime::now() and compute hours/minutes/seconds from UNIX_EPOCH duration. Format as HH:MM:SS string. (2) Render the time string centered horizontally at 35% screen height in ThermalPalette::WARM (#22c55e = [0.133, 0.773, 0.369, 1.0]), 72pt font size (large digital readout). (3) Below the time, render the date as 'YYYY-MM-DD' at 42% height in ThermalPalette::TEXT_MUTED (#7c6faa), 20pt. (4) Above the time at 28% height, render a small label 'THERMAL-LOCK' in ThermalPalette::ACCENT_COLD (#6366f1), 11pt, letter-spaced. (5) Use glyphon::Attrs to set the font family to monospace for all text. Update the time buffer each frame (or every second — track last_second: u64 in LockSurface and only re-layout when it changes). Dependencies: glyphon = '0.7', cosmic-text = '0.12'. No external time crate needed — use std::time. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.

title: Failed auth feedback
notes: FLASH_SHADER solid-color fullscreen pipeline (make_flash_pipeline()). flash_color_buf updated to CRITICAL at alpha 0.3 when shake_timer > 0. shake_offset = sin(shake_timer*40)*8 applied to masked input X position. delta_time tracked via last_frame: Instant in WgpuSurface. shake_timer decremented in main loop; failed=false and password cleared when timer hits 0. KeyboardHandler impl with SeatHandler; xkeysym::key::BackSpace/Return/KP_Enter handled. delegate_seat!/delegate_keyboard! added. cargo check passes.
prompt: In crates/thermal-lock/src/main.rs, implement visual feedback for failed authentication. (1) When authenticate() returns false, set AuthState.failed=true and AuthState.shake_timer=0.5 (seconds). (2) In render_frame(), if shake_timer > 0.0: subtract delta_time from shake_timer, compute shake_offset = sin(shake_timer * 40.0) * 8.0 pixels, apply this as a horizontal translation to the auth input glyphon buffer position. (3) While failed=true and shake_timer > 0.0, tint the entire background by blending ThermalPalette::CRITICAL (#dc2626 = [0.863, 0.149, 0.149, 1.0]) at alpha 0.3 over the heat-map output — implement this as a second fullscreen-quad render pass with additive/alpha blending using a solid-color pipeline. (4) After shake_timer reaches 0.0, set failed=false and clear the password buffer. (5) Track delta_time between frames using std::time::Instant stored in LockSurface as last_frame: Instant, computing elapsed each render call. Dependencies: wgpu = '24', glyphon = '0.7'. Success criteria: cargo check -p thermal-lock must pass.
